1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"

18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	// Git helpers
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}
59	
60	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	// Repo setup
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}

114	// writeDirectiveFile writes a directive file at path with the given lines.
115	func writeDirectiveFile(t *testing.T, path string, lines []string) {
116		t.Helper()
117		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755))
118		content := strings.Join(lines, "\n") + "\n"
119		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644))
120	}

1284			return env
1285		}
1286		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
1287			t.Helper()
1288			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
1289			cmd.Dir = workDir
1290			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
1291			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
1292			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
1293			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1294		}
1295	
1296		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
1297		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
1298		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
1299		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1300		runGit("add", ".")
1301		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1302	
1303		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1304			ID:        "my-bead",
1305			Title:     "Test --from flag resolves custom revision",
1306			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
1307			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
1308		})
1309		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1310		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1311	
1312		// Tag this commit as the custom ref --from will resolve against. The
1313		// worktree snapshot at this tag contains the bead. HEAD will advance past
1314		// this commit below, so BaseRev != HEAD unless --from is honored.
1315		customSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1316		runGit("tag", "custom-rev")
1317	
1318		// Advance HEAD past the custom ref with an unrelated commit so --from is
1319		// observable: if it were ignored, BaseRev would equal the newer HEAD.
1320		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "after.txt"), []byte("after\n"), 0o644))
1321		runGit("add", "after.txt")
1322		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: after tag")
1323	
1324		// no-op directive: agent makes no commits, so no merge logic is triggered.
1325		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1326		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte("no-op\n"), 0o644))
1327	
1328		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1329		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1330		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1331		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1332			"--from", "custom-rev",
1333			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1334		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
1335		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1336	
1337		assert.Equal(t, customSHA, res.BaseRev, "BaseRev must equal SHA resolved from --from tag")
1338	}
1339	
1340	// TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery verifies that worktrees matching the bead's
1341	// prefix are cleaned up at the start of a new run.
1342	func TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery(t *testing.T) {
1343		workDir := t.TempDir()
1344		orphanPath := workDir + "/.ddx/" + agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + "my-bead-old-attempt"

data.text: Committed. Migration complete: `TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery` now uses real git (via inline `runGit` helper) + real `agent.NewRunner` with the script harness. It registers a real orphan worktree via `git worktree add`, then asserts the orphan dir is removed and unregistered after the run. No other tests in `agent_execute_bead_test.go` were modified.
